statement.en: A truncated soil phage catechol 1,2-dioxygenase illustrates how viruses preserve and disseminate auxiliary catalytic functions in the soil microbiome
statement.summary: This repository’s source data are archived on Zenodo to provide a permanent, citable record with a DOI. The Zenodo record is the canonical citation for the dataset used in the manuscript, while the GitHub repository specified in the Code Availability section hosts the analysis code and documentation.

proposer: Ruonan Wu, Garry W. Buchko, Ryan McClure et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper dated 2026-04-25 (future date, likely typo or preprint staging). Core claim about viral auxiliary functions in soil microbiomes is empirically testable but appears in provisional/preprint form. Catechol dioxygenase truncation mechanism is CLASS-B (biochemistry) and validatable, but broader ecological claims about viral dissemination in microbiomes require field validation. Status: under-validated but not refuted.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: ウイルスが触媒を運ぶ速度は、地中の活性化障壁を越える確率に比例する。

visualGlyph.interpretation: ウイルスの粒子が土壌ネットワークを通じて酵素機能を螺旋状に拡散させる姿。
